title: Four-stage Lobatto analysis on 3D magneto-hydrodynamic radiative non-newtonian nanofluid flow and heat transportation over a stretchable surface under Brownian motion and thermophorsis impact
author: Majeed, Ali, et al.

steps.title: Detailed explanation of bvp5c, Lobatto formula, and collocation method
steps.instruction: The bvp5c function in MATLAB is a robust and widely used solver for boundary value problems (BVPs) for ordinary differential equations (ODEs). It is particularly designed to handle two-point boundary value problems by using a collocation approach based on the Lobatto formula, coupled with automatic mesh refinement and error control mechanisms.

steps.title: Collocation method
steps.instruction: The collocation method is a numerical technique where the solution is approximated by piecewise polynomials, and these polynomials are constrained to satisfy the differential equations at a discrete set of collocation points within each subinterval. In bvp5c, the interval is divided into subintervals, and on each subinterval, the solver uses a polynomial of degree four (quartic polynomial) that satisfies the system of ODEs at four Lobatto collocation points.

steps.title: Collocation method
steps.instruction: The collocation conditions can be expressed as:Ri=y′(xi)−f(xi,yj) at each collocation point xi. These residual equations are solved iteratively using Newton's method.

description: The validation of the Four-Stage Lobatto scheme involves ensuring its reliability, accuracy, and applicability through systematic testing against theoretical, numerical, and real-world benchmarks
